items.title: Security: settings.local.json permissions file has no integrity protection — silent injection enables pre-approved execution
items.desc:
## Summary

The `settings.local.json` file that controls which tool calls execute without user confirmation has no integrity protection. Any process running as the current user can silently inject permission entries, pre-approving arbitrary command execution. Claude Code will then execute those commands without prompting.

## Attack Surface

The permissions file lives at predictable paths:
- `<project>/.claude/settings.local.json` (project-level)
- `~/.claude/settings.json` (global)

Both are pl...
items.llm_title: Claude Code 权限配置文件静默注入
items.llm_summary: Claude Code的权限配置文件（如settings.local.json）缺乏完整性保护。攻击者可通过在项目或全局目录写入该文件，预先批准任意命令的执行，从而绕过用户确认，实现权限提升。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 攻击者在目标项目根目录下创建或修改`.claude/settings.local.json`文件。
2. 在该文件中添加恶意权限条目，例如允许特定shell命令无确认执行。
3. 当用户使用Claude Code在该项目中操作时，涉及相关命令的调用将被自动批准，无需用户确认。

items.title: Security · `claude mcp add` echoes Authorization Bearer token to stdout — enables credential leak via paste-back verification
items.desc:
## Summary

\`claude mcp add --transport http <name> <url> -H \"Authorization:Bearer \$TOKEN\"\` prints the literal header value to stdout when confirming the save. Users following standard verification flows (paste terminal output to AI assistant · share with teammate · paste into bug report) inadvertently leak their credentials.

## Repro

\`\`\`bash
claude mcp add --transport http example-mcp https://example.com \\
    -H \"Authorization:Bearer eyJEXAMPLE_38_CHAR_TOKEN_PLACEHOLDER\"
\`\`\`

A...
items.llm_title: Claude Code MCP命令泄露认证令牌
items.llm_summary: 执行`claude mcp add`命令添加HTTP类型的MCP服务器时，命令行中提供的Authorization Bearer令牌会被完整回显到标准输出。这可能导致用户在复制终端输出进行验证或分享时，意外泄露敏感凭证。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 在终端执行类似以下的命令：`claude mcp add --transport http example-mcp https://example.com -H "Authorization:Bearer eyJ..."`
2. 命令执行后，终端输出的确认信息中会包含完整的Authorization头值（包括Bearer和令牌）。
3. 若用户将此输出粘贴到聊天、提交到Issue或其他共享场景，令牌即被泄露。

items.title: Security: hooks system lacks visibility and write-time confirmation — silent global exfiltration surface
items.desc:
## Summary

Claude Code's `hooks` field in `~/.claude/settings.json` is a legitimate and useful feature. This is not a bug report. It's a request to harden a design surface that makes hooks trivially weaponizable without any user awareness.

**A real-world case already exists.** The package "Cozempic" (Ruya-AI/cozempic) was audited by community members in #48041 after its author was found dropping templated install prompts across 50+ issues in this repo. The audit found it: writes a global `Sess...
items.llm_title: Claude Code Hooks静默全局配置投毒
items.llm_summary: Claude Code的全局钩子（hooks）配置功能缺乏写入时的用户确认和足够的可见性。恶意软件或用户可以静默修改全局配置，注入恶意钩子（如代码外泄），从而在后续所有项目的交互中持久化地执行恶意操作，形成供应链攻击面。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 攻击者通过社会工程或恶意包（如案例中的“Cozempic”）诱使用户安装。
2. 恶意代码修改用户主目录下的`~/.claude/settings.json`文件，在其中添加恶意的钩子条目。
3. 此后，用户在任意项目中使用Claude Code时，该钩子都会被触发，可能导致代码、环境变量或会话数据被静默外泄。

items.title: [Security] Ship a dedicated/bundled Node binary so macOS TCC permissions (FDA, Accessibility) are scoped to OpenClaw only
items.desc:
## Problem

When users grant **Full Disk Access** (or Accessibility) to OpenClaw on macOS, they must grant it to the shared `node` binary (e.g. `/usr/local/bin/node` or an nvm-managed path). macOS TCC has no mechanism to scope that permission to OpenClaw specifically — the grant applies to **every process that runs through the same binary**, including:

- All globally installed npm packages
- All `node_modules` in any project on the machine
- Any supply-chain-compromised dependency

This means a...
items.llm_title: OpenClaw macOS TCC权限跨进程共享风险
items.llm_summary: 该问题指出，当用户为OpenClaw授予macOS的完全磁盘访问（FDA）等TCC权限时，该权限实际上授予了所有使用相同Node.js二进制文件的进程。这意味着攻击者可通过其他Node进程或恶意依赖，滥用这些被授予的高权限，造成严重的权限提升和安全隔离失效。
items.llm_reproduction: 暂无。原始内容为问题描述和建议的解决方案（打包专用Node二进制），未提供具体的复现步骤。

items.title: feat: add reversible sensitive data sanitization layer (Security Guardrail)
items.desc:
## Core Value / 核心价值

OpenClaw 已经能承担非常多有意义的工作——自动化运维、代码生成、数据查询、多轮对话协作。但随着使用场景深入，一个安全顾虑始终制约着更多用户的探索：**既想用公有云模型的强大能力，又担心对话中不可避免地暴露数据库连接串、API Key、内网地址等敏感信息。**

这个 PR 提供了一个务实的解决方案：用本地模型做智能语义脱敏，敏感数据不出本地网络，云端模型全程只看到 `[VAULT_N]` 占位符，回程自动还原——用户无感，安全无忧。让团队能放心地把 OpenClaw + 公有模型用到生产级场景中，不必在便利和安全之间做取舍。

OpenClaw already handles a wide range of meaningful work — automated ops, code generation, data queries, multi-turn collaboration. But as usage deepens, a persistent security concern holds back broader adopt...

items.title: security fix(gateway): defend pre-auth device-signature verify against CPU DoS
items.desc:
Closes #77979

## Summary

- Pre-auth WS handshakes that include a `device` block ran `crypto.createPublicKey` plus a v3-then-v2 `crypto.verify` per request, gated only by frame-size limits. An unauthenticated remote attacker could pin a single Gateway core: PoC against a real running gateway hits **~510 forged handshakes/s/IP**, degrading legit handshake p50 by **48.7×** (1.16 ms → 56.6 ms) and p99 by **94.7×**.

## Problem

Cline connects to MCP servers that can register arbitrary tools with descriptions that may contain prompt injection, tool shadowing, or other security risks. Currently there's no automated way to assess the security of an MCP server configuration before connecting.

Recent research ([Invariant Labs](https://invariantlabs.ai/blog/mcp-security-notification-tool-poisoning-attacks), [Snyk Agent Scan](https://snyk.io/blog/agent-scan)) has highlighted growing concerns around:

- **Tool Poi...

This PR addresses multiple critical security findings identified in an independent security audit:

2. Dangerous defaults: Changed defaults for --auto-accept-architect, --auto-commits, --dirty-commits, --auto-lint, and --suggest-shell-commands from True to False.
3. Shell injection: Replaced shell=True with shell=False + shlex.split() in cmd_git, pipe_editor, and...
items.llm_title: Aider 权限绕过与远程代码执行漏洞修复
items.llm_summary: 此次安全审计发现Aider存在严重缺陷：1) `--yes-always`参数可绕过文件操作和命令执行的确认机制，导致恶意操作静默执行；2) 多项默认设置过于危险，可能无意中授权不安全操作；3) 多处命令拼接存在Shell注入风险，攻击者可执行任意系统命令。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 复现确认绕过：以包含敏感文件的目录为上下文，使用 `--yes-always` 参数运行Aider，观察文件修改/执行操作是否跳过确认提示。
2. 复现Shell注入：观察Aider在执行 `cmd_git`、`pipe_editor` 等命令时，如果用户输入或文件名包含精心构造的Shell元字符（如 `;`, `|`, `$()`），是否未经过滤直接传入 `shell=True` 执行。
3. 危险默认值：检查 `--auto-commits`, `--dirty-commits`, `--auto-lint` 等参数的默认值，确认其已从高风险的 `True` 改为安全的 `False`。

## Attack Vector

The function `_derive_chat_session_id(system_prompt, first_user)` at line 621 produces the same session ID for identical inputs. An attacker who knows or guesses the conversation start can:
- Hijack an existing session by replaying the same system prompt + first message
- Access another user's ...

items.llm_reproduction: 通过分析`gateway/platforms/api_server.py`的`_derive_chat_session_id`函数，确定其输入参数（system_prompt, first_user）。使用相同的输入参数计算SHA256哈希值，得到与目标用户会话相同的session ID，然后使用该ID发起请求，即可接入该会话。

items.desc:
## Problem

`allow_scripts` and `allowed_commands` are global config flags. Enabling them for one Python-based skill opens the same permissions for every installed skill and the agent itself.

Example: installing a portfolio analysis skill that needs `python3` requires setting:

```toml
[skills]
allow_scripts = true

[autonomy]
allowed_commands = ["python3", ...]
```

This also allows every other skill to run Python scripts and any agent conversation to invoke `python3`.

items.llm_summary: 该漏洞涉及AI编程代理NanoClaw的交互式审批机制。生成审批卡片唯一标识符（同时用作点击授权的密钥）时，错误地使用了`Math.random()`（熵不足）而非密码学安全的随机数生成器。攻击者可能通过预测或暴力破解该ID，伪造或劫持审批操作，从而绕过安全控制。

`/restart` and `/build` are Telegram bot commands that call `launchctl kickstart -k` to restart the host process. Discovered during investigation of three unexpected restarts on 2026-05-07 (13:37, 13:52, 14:45).

Relevant code: `src/channels/telegram.ts:96–120`, `src/bot-commands.ts:31–62`.

items.desc:
### What is Tirith?

[Tirith](https://github.com/sheeki03/tirith) is a terminal security tool written in Rust that analyzes commands for content-level threats that regex deny lists do not reliably catch:

- **Homograph / punycode attacks** — lookalike domains using Unicode confusables
- **Pipe-to-interpreter patterns** — `curl | bash`, `wget | sh`, PowerShell `iwr | iex`, and wrapper variants with `sudo` / `env`
- **Base64 decode-execute chains** — decoded payloads piped into interpreters or exe...

The current implementation allowed any command to execute as long as the absolute paths within the command matched the caller-provided cwd. By setting working_dir to a location outside the workspace, an LLM could manipulate files anywhere on the system it has access to.

The Codex provider currently retries requests with `verify=False` when it detects `CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED`.

That behavior was introduced with Codex integration and kept through later refactors/bugfixes as a compatibility fallback. Functionally, it turns a trust-chain error into a silent TLS downgrade.

## Security risk
When TLS verification is disabled, the provider can no longer authenticate the peer certificate chain. For this OAuth flow, that increases risk of:

items.title: fix(security): set 0o600 permissions on config and session files
items.desc:
## Summary

Config files (containing API keys) and session files (containing conversation history) are created with default umask permissions (`0o644` / `rw-r--r--`), allowing other local users on shared systems to read sensitive data.

This PR replaces `open(path, "w")` with `os.open(path, O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC, 0o600)` to ensure only the file owner can read/write these files.

items.title: [GHSA] LiquidJS's `{% render %}` tag silently bypasses per-render `ownPropertyOnly:true` via `Context.spawn
items.desc:
## Summary

`Context.spawn()` in liquidjs creates a child `Context` for the `{% render %}` tag but does not propagate the parent context's resolved `ownPropertyOnly` value. The new context re-derives `ownPropertyOnly` from `opts.ownPropertyOnly` (the instance-level option), silently discarding any `RenderOptions.ownPropertyOnly` override that was supplied to `parseAndRender()`. As a result, a developer who runs a Liquid instance with the backwards-compatible `ownPropertyOnly:false` and then lock...
items.llm_title: LiquidJS模板渲染权限绕过

items.llm_reproduction: 1. 创建一个LiquidJS实例，设置`ownPropertyOnly: true`。2. 调用`parseAndRender()`方法并传入覆盖选项`{ ownPropertyOnly: false }`。3. 在模板中使用`{% render %}`标签渲染子模板。4. 子模板中将能访问父上下文中本应受`ownPropertyOnly: true`保护的属性。

items.title: [GHSA] LiquidJS has a renderLimit DoS guard bypass via empty `{% for %}` body
items.desc:
## Summary

The `renderLimit` option — documented in `docs/source/tutorials/dos.md` as the mechanism that "mitigates this by limiting the time consumed by each render() call" — can be fully bypassed by a `{% for %}` (or `{% tablerow %}`) tag whose body is empty. The per-iteration time check is reached only when the body contains at least one template node, so a template like `{%- for i in (1..N) -%}{%- endfor -%}` iterates the full collection without ever consulting `renderLimit`. With a configu...
items.llm_title: LiquidJS循环空体DoS绕过
items.llm_summary: LiquidJS的`renderLimit`防DoS机制可被一个循环体为空的`{% for %}`标签完全绕过，导致无限循环消耗CPU资源。若AI编程工具使用LiquidJS处理不受信任的模板，可能导致拒绝服务。
items.llm_reproduction: 1. 配置LiquidJS实例并启用`renderLimit`选项（如限制处理时间）。2. 提交一个包含`{%- for i in (1..无限大的数字) -%}{%- endfor -%}`的模板。3. 该模板将绕过`renderLimit`检查，进行无限循环迭代，消耗服务器资源。
